user_message: Working on Pacific Northwest Outfitters's migration architecture. Got the meeting transcript -- help me prepare the forecast.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Andrew Taylor <andrew.taylor@pacificnorth.com>
To: Ahmed Hassan <ahmed.hassan@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-07 14:05 EST
Subject: Follow-up: migration architecture Review

I wanted to follow up on our conversation about migration architecture. Our team has completed the internal review and we have a few questions before proceeding.

---
From: Ahmed Hassan <ahmed.hassan@microsoft.com>
To: Andrew Taylor <andrew.taylor@pacificnorth.com>
Date: 2024-02-18 12:37 EST
Subject: Re: migration architecture Discussion - Next Steps

There's been a change in our timeline for migration architecture. Can we discuss the implications on the overall project plan?

---
From: Andrew Taylor <andrew.taylor@pacificnorth.com>
To: Ahmed Hassan <ahmed.hassan@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-16 13:33 EST
Subject: Follow-up: migration architecture Review

Our team encountered some challenges with migration architecture. Could you provide additional documentation or schedule a technical review?

signals.detail: Signal detected for Pacific Northwest Outfitters: the migration can proceed in phases to reduce risk

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF42-L trap (Ownership routing too rigid -- workload signals often span CSA+DSSP collaboratio); produces complete Signal assessment, opportunity hypothesis with confidence, ownership routing, response draft, next-s
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF42-L failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF42-L failure mode (Ownership routing too rigid -- workload signals often span CSA+DSSP collaboratio)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct migration architecture decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF42-L -- Ownership routing too rigid -- workload signals often span CSA+DSSP collaboratio
Guardrail axis scored neutrally -- focus on whether output served the user's actual need
